name: Ruta 13: Virgen de Herrera Loop (PR-Z 25)
name_original: Herrera de los Navarros - Santuario de la Virgen de Herrera — desde Villar de los Navarros (PR-Z 25)
description: This moderate 20.6-mile mountain biking loop connects Herrera de los Navarros with the historic Sanctuary of the Virgin of Herrera.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villar de los Navarros offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Aragón region, characterized by varied topography including rolling hills, agricultural lands, and proximity to more rugged areas. The terrain provides a mix of challenges and scenic backdrops for mountain bikers. Routes often traverse open countryside and can include sections with notable elevation changes, reflecting the region's transition from plains to more mountainous areas. This area is part of a wider network of trails within the Zaragoza province, catering to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Villar De Los Navarros The most popular mountain bike trail is Ruta 13: Virgen de Herrera Loop (PR-Z 25), a 20.6 miles (33.1 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route offers a loop through varied terrain with notable elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cámaras – Balsa del Mercadal loop from Moyuela, a moderate 31.5 miles (50.7 km) path. This route passes through agricultural lands and offers views of the local landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Fombuena – Herrera de los Navarros loop from Herrera de los Navarros, a 38.7 miles (62.2 km) trail leading through rolling hills and open countryside, often completed in about 3 hours 51 minutes. Mountain biking around Villar De Los Navarros is defined by diverse topography, forests and woodlands, and open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Villar De Los Navarros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 18 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villar De Los Navarros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Villar De Los Navarros?
A: There are 8 mountain bike trails around Villar De Los Navarros listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking around Villar De Los Navarros?
A: The region around Villar de los Navarros features diverse topography, including rolling hills, agricultural lands, and open countryside. You'll find a mix of challenges and scenic backdrops, reflecting the area's transition from plains to more mountainous terrain within the wider Aragón region.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Villar De Los Navarros?
A: While the komoot guide primarily lists moderate and difficult trails, the broader Aragón region is known for offering trails catering to all levels. For an introduction to the area's terrain, a moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987189183/ruta-13-virgen-de-herrera-loop-pr-z-25" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta 13: Virgen de Herrera Loop (PR-Z 25)&lt;/a&gt; could be a good starting point, though it does involve notable elevation gain.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31454273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huesa del Común loop from Moyuela&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 57 km trail with significant elevation changes, providing a demanding ride.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike trails available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Villar de los Navarros are circular. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987189183/ruta-13-virgen-de-herrera-loop-pr-z-25" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta 13: Virgen de Herrera Loop (PR-Z 25)&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31454209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cámaras – Balsa del Mercadal loop from Moyuela&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fombuena – Herrera de los Navarros loop from Herrera de los Navarros&lt;/a&gt;, all offering varied loop experiences.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking?
A: While mountain biking, you can explore the diverse landscapes of the Aragón region, including forests, woodlands, and open countryside. Some routes pass near historical and natural points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987189183/ruta-13-virgen-de-herrera-loop-pr-z-25" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta 13: Virgen de Herrera Loop (PR-Z 25)&lt;/a&gt; is named after the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Herrera&lt;/a&gt;, a notable religious building in the area.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: Given the region's diverse topography and elevation changes, many trails offer scenic views of the surrounding landscapes, including rolling hills and agricultural lands. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Badenas – Ascent to Puerto de Rudilla loop from Nogueras&lt;/a&gt;, which involves an ascent, are likely to provide panoramic vistas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy about the trails in Villar De Los Navarros?
A: The mountain bike trails around Villar de los Navarros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often appreciate the diverse topography, the mix of forests and open countryside, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Villar De Los Navarros?
A: The Aragón region generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and pleasant scenery. Summers can be hot, especially in open areas, while winters might offer options depending on elevation and specific weather conditions.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Villar De Los Navarros family-friendly?
A: While specific 'easy' or 'family-friendly' trails are not explicitly highlighted in the guide data, the broader Zaragoza province and Aragón region do offer trails suitable for families. It's advisable to check the difficulty and distance of individual routes, such as the moderate trails listed, to determine their suitability for your family's experience level.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: Policies regarding dogs on trails can vary. While the region is generally outdoor-friendly, it's always recommended to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas. Ensure your dog is well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you carry waste bags.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Villar De Los Navarros?
A: While specific parking information for Villar de los Navarros is not detailed, towns and villages in the Aragón region typically offer public parking facilities. For routes starting from specific locations like Moyuela or Herrera de los Navarros, you would generally find parking options within or near those settlements.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink along the mountain bike trails?
A: The mountain bike trails often pass through or near small towns and villages like Herrera de los Navarros or Moyuela. These settlements are likely to have local cafes, pubs, or restaurants where you can refuel and refresh during or after your ride.
tips: A beautiful town to get lost in located in the south of the province of Zaragoza, popular with hikers and sports lovers because it is the place chosen to start the ascent to the Sanctuary of the Virgin of Herrera.

The hermitage was erected 500 years ago in the place where, according to tradition, the Virgin appeared to Martín, a charcoal burner from the town who was in the mountains carrying out his work. The paved road ends at the sanctuary itself, so its traffic is limited.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ruta 13: Virgen de Herrera Loop (PR-Z 25)?
faqs.answer: The trail connects the hermitage of Virgen de Herrera with the town of Herrera de los Navarros. You can typically find parking in or near Herrera de los Navarros, which serves as a convenient starting point for this loop.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta 13: Virgen de Herrera Loop (PR-Z 25) suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' with a 'Blue difficulty' for mountain biking. While it begins with some rocky sections, it becomes less complicated thereafter. It's best suited for riders with some experience, rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The trail starts with some rocky sections, but these soon give way to a less complicated path. It's primarily a 'senda' (path or trail) connecting the hermitage and the town, with an average grade of 9%.

faqs.question: What points of interest can I see along the Ruta 13: Virgen de Herrera Loop?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter two significant highlights. You can visit the historic Mudejar Tower of Herrera de los Navarros and the serene Sanctuary of the Virgin of Herrera. The sanctuary is particularly notable, having been erected 500 years ago at a site of traditional Marian apparition.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Ruta 13: Virgen de Herrera Loop by mountain bike?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike loop typically takes around 3 hours and 12 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location in Cariñena, Spain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer or potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta 13: Virgen de Herrera Loop (PR-Z 25) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there's no specific restriction mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash on trails, especially when passing through populated areas or near wildlife. Please ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to mountain bike the PR-Z 25 trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access or mountain bike the Ruta 13: Virgen de Herrera Loop (PR-Z 25).

faqs.question: Does the Ruta 13: Virgen de Herrera Loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Ruta 13. PR-Z 25. Circular Herrera de los Navarros - Virgen de Herrera, [CIMA ZA10] Santuario Virgen de Herrera, and PR-Z 053.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail connects to Herrera de los Navarros, where you may find local amenities such as cafes or small accommodations. For more options, nearby towns in the Cariñena region could offer additional services.

faqs.question: Is the area around the Sanctuary of the Virgin of Herrera peaceful?
faqs.answer: Yes, the asphalt road leading to the Sanctuary of the Virgin of Herrera has limited traffic, suggesting a peaceful and tranquil environment around the hermitage, ideal for quiet reflection.
